# leadhuman.ai

> Lead humanly. Learn constantly. Build with AI.

## About

leadhuman.ai is a thought leadership platform by Jay Vergara, an L&D strategist and cross-cultural communication specialist based in Tokyo, Japan. The site covers AI tutorials, leadership development, cross-cultural business, and organizational learning.

Jay is a Partner and Lead Learning Consultant at Peak Potential Consulting, with experience at Indeed, Rakuten, TELUS, and Yellow Pages. He is currently completing his MBA at GLOBIS University.

## Languages

The site is available in English (default) and Japanese:
- English: https://leadhuman.ai/
- Japanese: https://leadhuman.ai/ja/

All content, pages, and blog posts are fully translated and localized.

## Content

The site publishes two types of content:

### Build with AI (/build/)
Practical tutorials and workflow breakdowns for professionals using AI in their work.

- [How I Built a Council of AI Advisors (And Why You Should Too)](https://leadhuman.ai/build/council-of-ai-advisors/): What if instead of one AI chat for everything, you had a personal advisory council? Each specialist trained on your context, your voice, and your goals.
- [How to Use Claude to Build a Personal Knowledge System in 30 Minutes](https://leadhuman.ai/build/claude-personal-knowledge-system/): A tutorial on turning Claude into a personal knowledge management system that remembers your context, career goals, and writing voice.

### Lead Humanly (/lead/)
Insights on leadership, cross-cultural communication, L&D strategy, and how people actually grow at work.

- [Why Every Leader Needs to Understand AI (Even If They Never Write a Prompt)](https://leadhuman.ai/lead/why-leaders-need-ai/): You don't need to become an AI engineer. But you do need to understand what AI can and can't do well enough to make smart decisions.
- [AI Is Changing L&D Faster Than Most Leaders Realize](https://leadhuman.ai/lead/ai-changing-ld/): The real shift is much bigger than faster course descriptions. Organizations that figure it out in the next 18 months will have a significant advantage.
- [Why Your Proposal Died Before the Meeting Started](https://leadhuman.ai/lead/why-your-proposal-died/): In Japan the meeting isn't where decisions get made. It's where they get confirmed. The real work happened days ago.
- [The Words You Can't Translate Are the Cultural Keys](https://leadhuman.ai/lead/words-you-cant-translate/): Words like nemawashi, kuuki wo yomu, and amae carry centuries of cultural wisdom that one-word translations can't capture.
- [The Best Leaders I Know Talk Less Than Everyone Else](https://leadhuman.ai/lead/leaders-who-listen/): Listening isn't passive. It's the most underrated leadership behavior in organizations.
- [Your Team Wants the Hard Conversation You Keep Avoiding](https://leadhuman.ai/lead/hard-conversation/): Most managers think they're protecting their team by softening feedback. The research says the opposite.
- [When Your Quietest Team Members Have the Most to Say](https://leadhuman.ai/lead/quiet-team-members/): Silence in meetings doesn't mean disengagement. It often means the environment isn't designed for how they think.
- [Stop Asking for Feedback. Start Sharing It.](https://leadhuman.ai/lead/stop-asking-start-sharing-feedback/): What actually builds psychological safety is sharing the feedback you've already received.
- [The Part of Management AI Can't Automate](https://leadhuman.ai/lead/the-part-of-management-ai-cant-automate/): AI can automate workflows but it can't navigate the messy human dynamics that make teams actually work.
- [Your Team Is Using AI Behind Your Back](https://leadhuman.ai/lead/your-team-is-using-ai-behind-your-back/): 57% of employees hide their AI usage from employers. That's not a tech problem, it's a leadership problem.
- [The People AI Was Supposed to Replace Are Running It](https://leadhuman.ai/lead/the-people-ai-was-supposed-to-replace/): Middle managers aren't overhead. They're the translation layer that makes AI adoption actually work.
- [Your Team Isn't Broken, Your AI Rollout Is](https://leadhuman.ai/lead/your-team-isnt-broken-your-ai-rollout-is/): Most AI rollout failures aren't about the technology. They're about the gap between how leaders talk about AI and how teams experience it.
- [The Trust Gap Behind Every Failed AI Rollout](https://leadhuman.ai/lead/your-team-isnt-resisting-ai-theyre-resisting-you/): When teams resist AI, they're usually not resisting the technology. They're resisting how it was introduced.

## Content Pillars

Japan and Cross-Cultural Business, HR and Learning & Development, AI and Technology, Leadership and Management, Career and Entrepreneurship.
